P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 9580.4 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581.4 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
James J. Sheridan 
School Superintendent 
Administratian 
322·5660 
El Monte Hign School District 
Administration Building 
3537 Johnson Avenue 
El Monte, CA 
91731/3290 
Dear Mr. Sheridan: 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
Enf orcement 
322-6441 
December 20, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice, Our 
Advice No. A-83-274 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of the Board 
of Trustees of the El Monte Union Scnool District. 
Mr. Gutierrez, a member of the Board, nas applied for the 
position of Director of Work Experience, Career and Vocational 
Education for the District. 
Your question is whether 
Mr. Gutierrez can legally be considered for this position. 
The Political Reform Act does not prevent an official from 
applying for, or accepting, an additional governmental 
position. 
Tne conflict of interest provisions of the Act affect 
the official's decision making by prohibiting the official from 
making, participating in, or using his position to influence a 
governmental decision in which he knows, or nas reason to know, 
he has a financial interest. 
An official ha~ a "financial 
interest" in a decision if it is reasonaoly foreseeable that the 
decision will have a material financial effect on certain 
interests of the official. 
(Government Code Sections 87100 and 
87103. 1/ ) 
One of the interests referred to in Section 87l03(c) is a 
source of income to tne official aggregating $250 or more in 
value provided to the official within the 12 montns prior to the 
1/ Hereinafter, all statutory references are to the 
Govenrment Code unless otherwise indicated. 


James J. Sheridan 
December 20, 1983 
Page Two 
time when the decision is made. 
In the case of Mr. Gutierrez, 
the Board of Trustees is not a source of income to him, nor will 
the School District become a source of 1ncome to him if he 
accepts the pos1tion as Director, because salary from a local 
governmental entity is not income to the official (Section 
82030{bf(2) • 
Even though the Political Reform Act does not prohibit an 
official from holding two offices at the same time, there are 
some situations where such dual office holding is prohibited 
under the doctrine of incompatible offices. 
I have enclosed a 
sampling of Attorney General Opinions discussing this doctrine. 
Because the doctr1ne is outs1de of the authority of the Fair 
Political Practices Commission, I cannot advise you as to 
whether the two offices Mr. Gutierrez may hold are incom-
patiole. 
I suggest that you contact your District's legal 
counsel on this issue. 
If you have any additional queations concerning the 
poli tica 1 Reform Act·, please feel free to contact me at 
9l6/322-590l~ 
. 
JSM: km 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
g~/ift!t:!f:~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 


DISTRICT ADMINISTRATION 
OR. JAMES J. SHERIDAN 
Superintendent 
EI Monte Union High School District 
ADMINISTRATION BUILDING 
BOARD OF TRUSTEES 
MRS. BONNIE PERRIN 
PreSIdent 
OR. JACK W. WILUAMS 
AssIstant Superintendent 
Business 
3537 JOHNSON AVENUE. EL MONTE. CALIFORNIA 91731 /3290 
AREA CODE 213 575-2100 
MRS. DOROTHYL McCLANAHAN 
Clerk 
OR. EUGENE F. PLATZ 
AssIstant Superintendent 
Instruction 
MRS. HELEN T. ARCHER 
Member 
MR. RALPH GUTIERREZ 
Member 
MR. FRANK D. CLAYTON 
AsSIstant Supertntendent 
Personnel 
MRS. PATRICIA A. WALLACH 
Member 
December 7, 1983 
Fair Employment Practices Commission 
1100 "K" St reet 
Sacramento, California 95804 
ATTENTION LEGAL COUNSEL 
The Board of Trustees of the E1 Monte Union High School 
District requests a formal opinion concerning the follow-
ing issue: 
Ralph Gutierrez, a member of the Board of Trustees 
for the last two years, has applied for the position 
of Director of Work Experience, Career and Vocational 
Education within the district. 
I have been requested 
by the Board to seek an opinion on whether it is legal 
for Ralph Gutierrez as a board member to be a candidate 
for this position. 
Time is of the essence. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely, 
James J. Sheridan 
Oist,ict Superintendent

sections.question: Your question is whether Mr. Gutierrez can legally be considered for this position.
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not prevent an official from applying for, or accepting, an additional governmental position. In the case of Mr. Gutierrez, the Board of Trustees is not a source of income to him, nor will the School District become a source of income to him if he accepts the position as Director, because salary from a local governmental entity is not income to the official (Section 82030(b)(2)).
sections.question_synthetic: Can a school board member apply for and be considered for an additional position (Director of Work Experience, Career and Vocational Education) within the same school district without violating the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit Mr. Gutierrez from applying for or accepting the Director position, as the District salary is not considered income under the Act and no financial conflict of interest exists. However, the FPPC cannot advise on the separate doctrine of incompatible offices, which may apply independently.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Full request and response both present in document. Clear question and conclusion sections. Advice number A-83-274. Minor OCR artifacts (e.g., '9580.4' likely '95804'). Includes discussion of Government Code sections 87100, 87103, and 82030.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your question is whether Mr. Gutierrez can legally be considered for this position.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prevent an official from applying for, or accepting, an additional governmental position. In the case of Mr. Gutierrez, the Board of Trustees is not a source of income to him, nor will the School District become a source of income to him if he accepts the position as Director, because salary from a local governmental entity is not income to the official (Section 82030(b)(2)).

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to El Monte Union High School District's request regarding whether board member Ralph Gutierrez can legally apply for a Director position within the same district under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC concludes the Act does not prohibit such dual office holding but recommends District legal counsel review the doctrine of incompatible offices.
